title: The Jersey Stack — Ep 1: The Transistor (DRAFT v9 — 720p preview)
description:
🎬 DRAFT v9 (720p preview cut) — for review only, not for public sharing. Full 1080p master available locally; 720p uploaded for fast review.

New Jersey invented the transistor. A 20-mile stretch of NJ holds the birthplace of the thing that runs every device in your pocket, and a monument to itself just outside the Severance set. Episode 1 of The Jersey Stack follows the story from Bell Labs Murray Hill in 1947 through the Traitorous Eight walkout in 1957 — and ends back at Fort Monmouth, where Netflix is rebuilding the ground that built the hardware.

What changed v8 → v9:
• Scene 22 (InfoAge / VCF Museum) — narration corrected for historical accuracy. The earlier cut implied that 'some of the earliest transistors the Signal Corps ever bought' sit on display at InfoAge — that provenance is unverified. New narration credits the Vintage Computer Federation (VCF) Museum specifically (one of multiple museums on the InfoAge campus, distinct from the separate Signal Corps Museum) and frames the artifacts as 'examples of the earliest transistor-era technology.' WAV grew 38.1s → 54.2s.
• Scene 29 (Closing bookend / Bell Works → Netflix) — RESTORED the full closing montage that v8 had cut to a 7-word button. The Apr 29 narration regen had compressed this scene from 150 words to 'That is not a coincidence. That's continuity.' which left ~26 seconds of visuals running over silence. v9 restores the full Bell Works metroburb identity (2-million-square-foot, quarter-mile atrium open to the public) and the Bell Labs → computing → streaming → Netflix Fort Monmouth thesis. WAV grew 3.5s → 63.8s. Visuals now align beat-by-beat with narration: transistor monument drone → Bell Works exterior → atrium interior → groundbreaking ceremony → Netflix construction drone.
• Scene 4 (Title card) — re-rendered to remove dev metadata that had been baked into the preview frame. New version uses heavy serif main title (Georgia Bold, HBO-doc weight) over sans subtitle (Helvetica Neue Medium), thin documentary-blue rules above and below, no preview brackets.

What changed v7 → v8 (still in this cut):
• Scene 7 — new opening AI image visualizing the 'solid-state' bet (vacuum tube vs. germanium crystal on the same bench)
• Scene 13 — new AI imagery for Bardeen's surface-state insight and the November–December 1947 push at Lab 1E-455 in Murray Hill
• Scene 22 (image side) — archival 1946 photo of the Project Diana antenna at Camp Evans (Wikimedia, PD-USGov)
• Scene 24 — new AI image of a 1956 federal courtroom for the AT&T consent decree beat

What changed v6 → v7 (still in this cut):
• Scene 25 + 26 merged into a single Diaspora beat (~70s) with the new thesis ending: 'Silicon Valley came from New Jersey. California scaled it. But Jersey built it.'

What changed v5 → v6 (still in this cut):
• Pacing fix — every multi-image scene now stretches each panel to fill its full narration duration; xfade math no longer eats 0.5s per transition.

Known issues still in this cut:
• Scene 27 motion-graphic counter (1 → 92,000,000,000) still not rendered — static images stand in
• Some AI-generated text (S20 newspaper, S26 logo cascade) may have rendering artifacts

Chapters:
0:00 Cold Open — The Severance Hook
0:48 The Cathedral (Bell Labs Murray Hill)
2:42 The Problem (Vacuum Tubes)
3:52 The Trio (Bardeen, Brattain, Shockley)
5:42 December 23, 1947 — The Bench
7:18 The Fracture (Shockley Finds Out)
8:56 The Announcement & Cold War Customer
11:11 1956 — Nobel and the Decree
12:11 The Diaspora
13:19 What It Became (1 → 92 Billion)
13:54 Closing Bookend (Bell Works → Netflix Fort Monmouth)
14:58 Next Episode — The Switch

---
Part of The Jersey Stack — a five-episode series on how New Jersey invented the modern computer.
